examples.title: Scrum Sprint Planning Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm representing Sprint Planning ceremony in Scrum
examples.code:
SPRINT PLANNING
BEGIN
 INPUT: Product Backlog, Sprint Goal
 
 // Select items for Sprint
 Sprint Backlog = EMPTY
 Remaining Capacity = Sprint Duration
 
 WHILE Product Backlog NOT EMPTY AND Capacity > 0 DO
 SELECT highest priority item from Product Backlog
 IF item fits in Capacity THEN
 ADD item to Sprint Backlog
 Capacity = Capacity - item.estimate
 ELSE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Create tasks for Sprint Backlog items
 FOR EACH item in Sprint Backlog DO
 tasks = DECOMPOSE(item into tasks)
 ADD tasks to Sprint Backlog
 END FOR
 
 COMMIT to Sprint Backlog
 COMMIT Sprint Goal
 
 OUTPUT: Sprint Backlog, Sprint Goal
END
examples.explanation: Sprint Planning selects items from Product Backlog based on priority and team capacity. Items are decomposed into tasks. The team commits to a Sprint Goal and Sprint Backlog for the duration of the sprint.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) - depends on backlog size

examples.title: Spiral Model Iteration
examples.description: Algorithm showing one iteration cycle of the Spiral Model
examples.code:
SPIRAL ITERATION
BEGIN
 INPUT: Previous Risk Analysis, Current Requirements
 
 // Phase 1: Planning
 Determine objectives for this spiral
 Identify constraints and risks
 Create project plan for this phase
 
 // Phase 2: Risk Analysis
 risks = IDENTIFY_RISKS(objectives)
 FOR EACH risk in risks DO
 risk.impact = ASSESS(risk)
 IF risk.impact > Threshold THEN
 CREATE prototype to mitigate risk
 EVALUATE prototype
 UPDATE risk analysis
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Phase 3: Engineering
 IF risks are acceptable THEN
 Develop and test components
 Create deliverable prototype
 END IF
 
 // Phase 4: Evaluation
 customer = EVALUATE(deliverable)
 IF customer satisfied THEN
 EXIT with final product
 ELSE
 UPDATE requirements for next spiral
 RETURN to Phase 1 with updated scope
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: Each spiral iteration goes through four phases: Planning (objectives and constraints), Risk Analysis (identifying and mitigating risks), Engineering (development), and Evaluation (customer feedback). The spiral continues until customer is satisfied.

examples.title: Continuous Integration Pipeline
examples.description: CI/CD pipeline algorithm for DevOps practice
examples.code:
CI/CD PIPELINE
BEGIN
 EVENT: Developer commits code
 
 // Stage 1: Build
 trigger = TRUE
 WHILE trigger = TRUE DO
 pull latest code from repository
 RUN automated build script
 IF build succeeds THEN
 trigger = FALSE
 ELSE
 NOTIFY developer
 EXIT with failure
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Stage 2: Unit Tests
 RUN unit tests
 IF all tests pass THEN
 CONTINUE to next stage
 ELSE
 NOTIFY developer
 EXIT with failure
 END IF
 
 // Stage 3: Integration Tests
 RUN integration tests
 IF all tests pass THEN
 CONTINUE to deployment
 ELSE
 NOTIFY developer
 EXIT with failure
 END IF
 
 // Stage 4: Deploy to Staging
 DEPLOY to staging environment
 RUN smoke tests
 
 // Stage 5: Deploy to Production (CD)
 IF configuration.allow_auto_deploy THEN
 DEPLOY to production
 MONITOR application metrics
 IF errors detected THEN
 ROLLBACK to previous version
 END IF
 ELSE
 WAIT for approval
 IF approved THEN
 DEPLOY to production
 END IF
 END IF
 
 REPORT pipeline results
END
examples.explanation: This CI/CD pipeline shows automated stages from code commit through build, various test stages, staging deployment, and finally production deployment with rollback capability for quick recovery from issues.
